note: JOIN KEY 1/4 — normalized before any join (2004-2006 pad ids differently across modules)

THE WEIGHT FOR THIS MODULE, and it is NOT factor07. CONFIRMED against INEI's published illiteracy: factora07 gives 5.55 / 4.78 / 4.65 for 2019 / 2023 / 2024 against official 5.6 / 4.8 / 4.6. Using factor07 instead gives 7.29 / 6.34 / 6.33 — an inflation of ~1.5pp, a 32% relative error. Every education statistic must use this weight.
household expansion factor, carried in this module. Do NOT use it for education rates — see weight_education.

p302 is 'sabe leer y escribir' but it is a CONDITIONAL question: only ~33k of 108k people are asked (INEI skips it when schooling already implies literacy), so 75k are null. Illiterate = answered NO. Everyone else in the universe is literate, INCLUDING the skipped. Dividing by respondents only gives 40% illiteracy instead of 4.8%.
the official rate is over the 15+ population. Anchor: 4.78% in 2023 vs INEI 4.8% (weight_education, ages 15+).
last level approved. 1-11 stable 2004-2025; CODE 12 (basica especial) ADDED in 2020 — a new category appended, not a remap, so pooling is safe as long as 12 is handled.

mother tongue, from INEI's value labels. 1=Quechua 2=Aimara 3=Otra lengua nativa 4=Castellano are STABLE across the whole span, so INDIGENOUS = 1-3 pools safely 2004-2025. Beyond that the set MOVES: 5=Ingles exists in 2004 and disappears later, and 9=Lengua de senas peruanas plus codes 10-15 appear from 2020. 8 is 'es sordo mudo' in 2004 and 'No escucha/no habla' in 2025 — same concept, reworded.
enrolled this year in a basic/higher education programme
currently attending. INEI's attendance rate = enrolled AND attending.

received a literacy programme in the last 12 months: 1=Si, 2=No. INTERMITTENT — it is NOT collected every year (absent in 2025, for instance), so it resolves to a marked all-NA column in the years INEI did not ask it. Counts are marginal where it exists (51 yes in 2023): a flag, not a population estimate.
